# noaa-recon-api

> Open-source HTTP API serving archival GOES satellite imagery and NOAA Tail
> Doppler Radar (TDR) data as georeferenced map tiles, plus a raw-netCDF
> passthrough for client-side rendering. CORS-open, no auth, free to call
> from any web app.

Live base URL: https://joshmurdock.net/api
Local dev base URL: http://127.0.0.1:8000
OpenAPI schema (machine-readable, authoritative): {base}/openapi.json
Interactive docs: {base}/docs

## Live endpoints

- GET /v1/health: Liveness check. Returns {"status":"ok"}.
- GET /v1/satellite/tile?time=ISO8601Z&band=13|9&cmap=bd|enhanced|nrl|grayscale: Requests a georeferenced GOES IR/WV PNG tile for the nearest actual satellite scan to an arbitrary UTC timestamp (~10-min resolution). Async: first call returns {"status":"generating","key":...}; poll the status endpoint below until ready. Full param/response reference: API.md#get-v1satellitetile
- GET /v1/satellite/status/{key}: Poll for a tile job's result. Returns status ready|generating|error|idle, and on ready: png_url (relative — prefix with the base URL), bounds (Leaflet-format [[lat_s,lon_w],[lat_n,lon_e]]), band, cmap, satellite, scan_start.
- GET /demo/netcdf-three/: Static demo page rendering netCDF data client-side via Three.js/WebGL (netcdf-three library). Currently loads sample data only.

## Planned endpoints (return 501 today, shape documented for forward compatibility)

- GET /v1/tdr/missions, GET /v1/tdr/sweep: Tail Doppler Radar data from NOAA's raw aircraft archive. Not yet implemented — see API.md and app/services/tdr.py for the planned design.
- GET /v1/raw/netcdf: Server-side netCDF subsetting by center/dimensions for client-side rendering. Not yet implemented — see API.md and app/routers/raw.py.
- GET /v1/satellite/tile with band=2 (visible) or a geocolor product: Not yet accepted — only band=13 (Clean IR) and band=9 (Water Vapor) work today.

## Full documentation

- [API.md](API.md): Complete endpoint reference, response shapes, color table descriptions, and copy-pasteable integration examples in curl, JavaScript (Leaflet), and Python.
- [README.md](README.md): Setup, architecture, deployment, and contributor/agent notes (roadmap, conventions to keep, a documented reprojection bug already found and fixed).

## Integration quick-start (JavaScript)

```javascript
const data = await fetch('https://joshmurdock.net/api/v1/satellite/tile?time=' + new Date().toISOString() + '&band=13').then(r => r.json());
// poll https://joshmurdock.net/api/v1/satellite/status/{data.key} every ~3s until status is "ready",
// then: L.imageOverlay('https://joshmurdock.net/api' + data.png_url, data.bounds).addTo(map);
```

## Notes

- No auth, no API key, no enforced rate limit. Avoid polling status faster than every 3s.
- Identical requests (same band/cmap/satellite/resolved-scan-time) hit a server-side cache — cheap to repeat.
- License: MIT.
